visual_elements.description: Matrix Multiplication - The Process and Products
Start with two matrices A (of dimensions m x n) and B (n x p). Matrix multiplication is possible when the number of columns in A equals the number of rows in B.
- Multiply each element from row i by its corresponding column j, then sum all these products. This resultant summation forms a single entry for position [i][j] on matrix C (the product).
Perform this operation iteratively across the dimensions of A and B to populate every element in resulting matrix C.
- Matrix multiplication is associative but not commutative, hence order matters; AB may differ from BA.
